frameworks.name: rate-distortion theory
frameworks.one_line: Formalizes memory as a channel trading information rate against distortion

frameworks.name: neural population coding
frameworks.one_line: Population code realizes the abstract rate-distortion computation

core_question: How can rate-distortion theory of memory be realized by neural population coding to explain visual working memory?
key_assumptions: Memory acts as a noisy channel with limited information rate
Population coding implements the rate-distortion trade-off
manipulations: Linked computational-level rate-distortion model to a population coding implementation
Reanalyzed monkey prefrontal recordings during oculomotor delayed response
key_findings: Model reproduces key regularities of visual working memory
Model explains regularities not previously captured by population coding accounts
Reanalysis of monkey prefrontal neurons confirms a novel model prediction
interpretations: Rate-distortion theory bridges computational-level memory accounts and neural population coding
Working memory limits reflect a rate-distortion trade-off implemented in prefrontal populations
